_meta.description: Bundled offline glossary of common medical terms with plain-language (approx. B1) English definitions. Used to ground key-term definitions so they are not invented by the model. Extend freely; keys must be lowercase.
_meta.license: CC0 - definitions are original plain-language summaries.

terms.hypertension: High blood pressure. It means the force of blood pushing against your artery walls is higher than it should be.
terms.hypotension: Low blood pressure. It means the force of blood in your arteries is lower than normal.
terms.myocardial infarction: A heart attack. It happens when blood flow to part of the heart is blocked and heart muscle is damaged.
terms.angina: Chest pain or tightness caused by reduced blood flow to the heart.
terms.arrhythmia: An irregular heartbeat - the heart beats too fast, too slow, or unevenly.
terms.atrial fibrillation: A common type of irregular, often fast heartbeat that can raise the risk of stroke.
terms.ischemia: Reduced blood flow to a part of the body, so it does not get enough oxygen.
terms.ischaemia: Reduced blood flow to a part of the body, so it does not get enough oxygen.
terms.atherosclerosis: A build-up of fatty deposits inside the arteries that narrows them and slows blood flow.
terms.thrombosis: A blood clot that forms inside a blood vessel and can block blood flow.
terms.embolism: A blockage in a blood vessel caused by a clot or other material that has travelled there.
terms.edema: Swelling caused by extra fluid trapped in the body's tissues.
terms.oedema: Swelling caused by extra fluid trapped in the body's tissues.
terms.diabetes mellitus: A condition where blood sugar (glucose) levels are too high because the body cannot use insulin properly.
terms.type 2 diabetes: A common form of diabetes where the body does not respond well to insulin, keeping blood sugar high.
terms.hba1c: A blood test that shows your average blood sugar over the past two to three months.
terms.hyperlipidemia: High levels of fats, such as cholesterol, in the blood.
terms.cholesterol: A fatty substance in the blood. Too much of the 'bad' type can clog arteries.
terms.hypothyroidism: An underactive thyroid gland that makes too little thyroid hormone, which can cause tiredness and weight gain.
terms.hyperthyroidism: An overactive thyroid gland that makes too much thyroid hormone.
terms.anemia: A low level of red blood cells or haemoglobin, which can make you feel tired and short of breath.
terms.anaemia: A low level of red blood cells or haemoglobin, which can make you feel tired and short of breath.
terms.hemoglobin: The protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen around the body.
terms.haemoglobin: The protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen around the body.
terms.leukocytosis: A higher-than-normal number of white blood cells, often a sign the body is fighting infection.
terms.thrombocytopenia: A low number of platelets, the blood cells that help you stop bleeding.
terms.pneumonia: An infection that inflames the air sacs in the lungs, which may fill with fluid.
terms.bronchitis: Inflammation of the airways in the lungs, usually causing coughing.
terms.asthma: A condition where the airways narrow and swell, making it hard to breathe.
terms.copd: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease - a long-term lung condition that makes breathing difficult.
terms.dyspnea: Shortness of breath or difficulty breathing.
terms.dyspnoea: Shortness of breath or difficulty breathing.
terms.hypoxia: Not enough oxygen reaching the body's tissues.
terms.sepsis: A serious, life-threatening reaction to an infection that can damage organs.
terms.gastroenteritis: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, often causing diarrhoea and vomiting.
terms.hepatitis: Inflammation of the liver, often caused by a virus.
terms.cirrhosis: Serious scarring of the liver that stops it working properly.
terms.nephropathy: Damage to or disease of the kidneys.
terms.dialysis: A treatment that filters waste from the blood when the kidneys cannot.
terms.benign: Not cancer; not harmful and unlikely to spread.
terms.malignant: Cancerous; able to grow and spread to other parts of the body.
terms.carcinoma: A type of cancer that starts in the skin or the tissue lining organs.
terms.metastasis: The spread of cancer from where it started to another part of the body.
terms.biopsy: A test where a small piece of tissue is removed and examined for disease.
terms.tumor: A lump or growth of abnormal cells, which may be harmless or cancerous.
terms.tumour: A lump or growth of abnormal cells, which may be harmless or cancerous.
terms.lesion: An area of tissue that has been damaged or changed by injury or disease.
terms.inflammation: The body's response to injury or infection, causing redness, warmth, and swelling.
terms.fracture: A break or crack in a bone.
terms.osteoporosis: A condition where bones become weak and more likely to break.
terms.arthritis: Inflammation of one or more joints, causing pain and stiffness.
terms.stroke: A sudden interruption of blood flow to the brain that can damage brain cells.
terms.seizure: A sudden burst of electrical activity in the brain that can affect movement or awareness.
terms.migraine: A strong, often one-sided headache, sometimes with nausea and sensitivity to light.
terms.syncope: Fainting - a brief loss of consciousness caused by a drop in blood flow to the brain.
terms.acute: Coming on suddenly and lasting a short time.
terms.chronic: Lasting a long time or coming back again and again.
terms.prognosis: The likely course and outcome of a disease.
terms.diagnosis: Identifying which disease or condition explains a person's symptoms.
terms.etiology: The cause or origin of a disease.
terms.prophylaxis: Treatment given to prevent a disease rather than to treat it.
terms.contraindication: A reason why a particular treatment should not be used because it could be harmful.
terms.analgesic: A medicine that relieves pain, such as paracetamol or ibuprofen.
terms.antibiotic: A medicine that fights bacterial infections.
terms.anticoagulant: A medicine that helps prevent blood clots, sometimes called a blood thinner.
terms.hypertensive: Relating to or having high blood pressure.
terms.npo: Nothing by mouth - you should not eat or drink, often before a procedure.
terms.prn: As needed - take the medicine only when you need it.
terms.tid: Three times a day (a dosing instruction).
terms.stat: Immediately (an instruction to do something right away).
